text: This paper analyzes about the principle of popular sovereignty in Indonesian local election according to the 1945 Constitution. Until now the constitution interpretation in article 18 paragraph (4) about the word “democratically elected” that has not been decided yet, there are many interpretations from several groups. Some of them argue that elections must be held directly by the people and another one argues that elections can be held by regional parliament through a voting process. Based on several constitutional sections about the electoral system in Indonesia, the electoral system in local election is not the same as with national electoral system, namely the presidential and legislative elections. Through the constitutional analysis in this paper, it is stated that the representative election system which is carried out by the regional parliament is a democratic electoral process for upholding the principle of popular sovereignty with Pancasila values as its reference, the same intention with Indonesia’s founding fathers.
